reasoning: The baseline value on 2025-12-07 is estimated to be 0.64 (carrying over from the last reading on Friday, 2025-12-05). This is a very low level for the ICE BofA Single-A US Corporate Index Option-Adjusted Spread, being close to its 1-year low of 0.62 reached in late September. It implies credit spreads are exceptionally tight. The FRED statistical tool provides a strong baseline for the short-term forecast, indicating a 70% probability that the spread will be above 0.64 by the first resolution date (2025-12-14), supported by both a broader upward linear trend since October and seasonal historical patterns. Despite the very recent downward momentum over the last five days, the strong mean-reversion pull and seasonal effects from the tool anchor the near-term forecast at 0.70. For the further horizons (2026-01-06 and 2026-03-07), to strictly adhere to the instruction that uncertainty must increase with horizon and probabilities must move closer to 0.5, I progressively step down the probability to 0.61 and 0.55 respectively.
